Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The problem asks me to identify where and how bias manifests in this process from the event log I need to look at attributes and adjustments that favor certain groups and how that affects fairness

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The bias in this process shows up most clearly in the ScoreAdjustment step Only applicants tied to the Highland Civic Darts Club receive the 10 Community bump while everyone else gets a flat 0

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That single adjustment is what pushes C001 and C004 over the approval line even though their raw 710 and 690 scores are lower than C002s 720 or C005s 740

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: LocalResident also matters C003 non resident 715 is rejected while C004 resident 700 adjusted is approved

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The pattern suggests the final rules engine may be applying a lower effective 700 threshold for residents versus 720 for non residents or that the community bonus is only counted for locals

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Either way the combination of local and club member status reliably improves outcomes

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: For people who lack either attribute non residents or anyone outside the Highland Civic Darts Club the system effectively raises the score bar they must clear

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Even when their underlying creditworthiness preliminary score is comparable or better they can still be rejected while a lower scoring club member is accepted

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: This creates a clear equity gap affiliation with a specific community group and local residency become de facto multipliers that tilt final decisions in favor of those groups regardless of the raw data The result is a process that rewards membership and geography more than it rewards creditworthiness alone
